Keelhouse

lean macOS workbench for Jason's real workflow — explorer + editor + browser + real CLI agents, without IDE bloat · 68 slices · 6 shipped

Open project folder → inspect/edit files → preview web output → run one or more real Claude/Codex CLI terminals in that project → switch projects without separate heavy VS Code windows. Preserve VS Code muscle memory for supported explorer/editor/browser/terminal workflows; skip plugins and IDE bloat. Product name: Keelhouse; repo/package slug: agent-cli.

Terminal + processProjects + sessionsFiles + editorChrome + interactionQuality + shippingResearch & spikes🔒 launch blocker

Done

Rocks · foundational & risky

MAPPROOT · Promote app into real root/packageQuality + shipping
v0🔒

Turn the proven spike into the actual app package: stable naming, app identifier, scripts, dev/build paths, and repo layout. Stop treating the working terminal loop as a side experiment.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: app builds/runs from app/; npm package, Rust crate, and Tauri product identifier are renamed; README/ROADMAP point to app/; runtime smoke launched target/debug/agent-cli and spawned claude in the selected cwd.

MOPEN-FOLDER · Native open-folder workspaceProjects + sessions
v0🔒

Native folder picker creates the current workspace, matching the actual VS Code workflow: open app, pick Project A, terminal starts in Project A.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: first launch opens a native dialog; selected folder becomes the app workspace; terminal cwd matches it; Cmd+O switches folders.

MAGENT-LAUNCH · Launch real Claude in selected folderTerminal + process
v0🔒

Replace the current hardcoded login shell with a real agent process launched in the selected workspace. Start with Claude as the v0 default because it is the primary observed workflow.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: after folder selection/relaunch, one pane launches `claude` in that cwd from a persisted launch profile; Claude's TUI renders in the canvas.

MTERMINAL-HARDEN · Terminal component hardening passTerminal + process
v0

Close the remaining gaps between 'spike works' and 'can daily-drive one agent': resize behavior, scrollback basics, keyboard audit, copy/selection edge cases, and fast-output smoke tests.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: Ghostty viewport scrollback renders, Shift+PageUp/PageDown and wheel scrolling work, resize clamps dimensions, 300-line fast output stays usable, and copy-selection still works.

MPROCESS-ENV · Agent launch env and auth checksTerminal + process
v0🔒

Claude/Codex must launch with the same PATH/auth expectations as a normal terminal. Detect missing binaries, auth failures, and bad cwd before dumping the user into a dead pane.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: workspace cwd and command are preflighted before pane replacement; missing command renders a visible error with no child process; process exit emits a banner for runtime/auth-style failures; Claude still launches as a direct child in the selected cwd.

MAPP-SHELL · Lean app shell: rail/editor/terminal layoutChrome + interaction
v0_5🔒

Build the shape that replaces VS Code for this workflow: left file rail, main editor area, bottom/right terminal pane area. No marketing page, no extra IDE chrome.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: one project opens into a stable three-part shell with left file rail surface, editor surface, and bottom terminal panel; terminal resize uses the measured terminal panel; build/tests pass; real Tauri smoke launched Claude in the selected cwd.

MFILE-RAIL · File explorer railFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The VS Code feature Jason actually uses: a project tree on the left. Keep it dense, fast, and boring.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: backend lists the selected workspace with ignore::WalkBuilder, gitignore/app noisy-folder filtering, and an 8000-entry cap; frontend renders a dense React Arborist tree, directories expand/collapse, and files activate into the editor surface.

MFILE-WATCHER · File watcher and ignore rulesFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The rail must reflect real project changes without drowning in node_modules, target, build output, or ignored files.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: debounced notify watcher emits workspace-tree-changed for relevant paths only; frontend refreshes the rail from list_workspace_tree; noisy paths are unit-tested; visual smoke showed a newly created top-level directory appear in the rail after launch.

LEDITOR · CodeMirror file editorFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Open files from the rail into a real editor pane. This is not a decorative preview; it is the VS Code/Codex editor surface Jason actually uses.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: CodeMirror opens files from the rail with reliable pointer-down activation, line numbers, path context, TS/TSX/JS/JSX/MD syntax modes, dirty/saved status, Save button, and Cmd+S/Ctrl+S. Native UI smoke opened, edited, saved, and confirmed disk contents changed for a real workspace file.

MEDITOR-PARITY-UX · Robust editor parity UXFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Match the coding editor orientation shown in the reference: dense file tree beside a real source editor with path context, gutter, dirty state, restored view position, and active-file reveal.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: editor shows breadcrumb path context, stable CodeMirror line gutter, selected file highlight, active-file reveal via React Arborist scrollTo, restored per-file selection/scroll state, dirty markers in editor and rail, and screenshot QA artifacts for selected, dirty, save-error, no-file, missing, and 1024x640 narrow states in docs/qa/editor-parity/.

Pebbles · features

SLAST-WORKSPACE · Persist and reopen last workspaceProjects + sessions
v0🔒

Quit/relaunch should not make the user pick the same project again. Store the last workspace path in a small local persistence layer.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08 for the current shell-pane default: relaunch reopens the last folder and starts the pane there. Recheck after AGENT-LAUNCH changes the default command.

SDATA-STORAGE · Local state storage and resetProjects + sessions
v0

Define where workspace/session state lives and how to reset it when the app gets confused.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: docs/local-state.md documents the Tauri Store path, workspace.json schema, reversible reset command, and default launchProfile repair path.

SRECENT-PROJECTS · Recent projects switcherProjects + sessions
v0_5

Opening the same active projects repeatedly should be cheap, not a folder-picker ceremony every time.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: recentFolders persists, dedupes, caps, and prunes missing folders; the rail lists recent projects with an explicit Switch affordance; real Tauri smoke switched from Keelhouse to postures and persisted postures as the active folder.

SACTIVE-FILE-SYNC · Active file sync across rail and editorFiles + editor
v0_5

The user must always know which file is active. File rail selection, editor tab title, path bar, watcher refreshes, and workspace restore need to agree.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: activeFileByWorkspace persists by canonical workspace root; relaunch restores the saved file into rail selection, editor tab/path, and content; stale paths are cleared; watcher refresh reconciles the selected file to the fresh tree node when it still exists and preserves the missing-file warning when it disappears. Native screenshot proof: docs/qa/active-file-sync/restore.png.

SEDITOR-VIEW-STATE · Per-file editor view stateFiles + editor
v0_5

Switching files should not throw away the user's place. Cursor, selection, scroll, and focus are part of the editor experience.

Done criteria

Verified 2026-07-08: per-file view state captures selection anchor/head, scrollTop, and focus intent. Native smoke set first.ts to Ln 5, Col 9, switched to second.ts, switched back, restored the same line/column, then ArrowDown advanced to Ln 6, Col 1, proving CodeMirror focus was restored. Proof screenshots: docs/qa/editor-view-state/restore.png and docs/qa/editor-view-state/focus-restored.png.

Now

Rocks · foundational & risky

MDIRTY-DRAFT-PROTECTION · Dirty draft protectionFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

The userflow audit found the main trust risk: unsaved work can be lost when changing files/projects. This must be blocked before the editor feels real.

Done criteria

Unsaved editor buffers are preserved when switching files or projects. Closing/replacing a dirty buffer requires Save, Discard, or Cancel with the file name and consequence shown. Dirty drafts survive failed saves and never get cleared by navigation.

Next

Rocks · foundational & risky

MSAVE-CONFLICTS · External change and save conflict handlingFiles + editor
v0_5🔒

Agents and terminals can edit files while the editor is open. The app must not silently overwrite external changes.

Done criteria

If a file changes on disk after opening, the editor warns before overwrite and offers reload/overwrite choices.

MTERMINAL-ROBUSTNESS · Robust terminal parityTerminal + process
v0_5🔒

The terminal is the trusted agent surface, so it needs daily-driver robustness beyond the initial working loop.

Done criteria

Single-pane terminal verifies Claude/Codex TUIs, alternate screen apps, ANSI/truecolor styling, resize, scrollback, mouse selection, copy, paste, bracketed paste, common keyboard chords, command/cwd/status header, fast-output responsiveness, and screenshot/runtime QA.

MPROJECT-RAIL · Left project rail for open workspacesProjects + sessions
v1🔒

The core multi-project workflow: Project A, Project B, Project C live in one app instead of separate VS Code windows. Use a persistent left project/workspace rail, similar in spirit to Codex's sidebar, rather than relying only on top tabs.

Done criteria

Open at least 3 folders in a left project rail; each project shows active/running/exited/attention state; switching projects restores that project's session list, file rail, editor tabs, browser preview, terminal panes, and focused pane.

MPROJECT-SESSIONS · Project session rowsProjects + sessions
v1🔒

Translate Codex chat rows into named task/workbench sessions under each project. These are not editor tabs and not custom chat threads; they are saved work contexts for agent work.

Done criteria

A project can show multiple named sessions with recency/status, active highlight, Show more collapse, rename/delete controls, and running/exited/attention badges; selecting a session restores its editor tabs, browser URL, pane layout, pane labels/status, and transcript references without pretending dead processes are live.

MBROWSER-PREVIEW · Browser and web preview paneChrome + interaction
v1🔒

The stripped VS Code workbench still needs a browser surface for localhost apps, docs, auth flows, and agent-generated pages without context switching.

Done criteria

A project can open a browser/web preview pane with address/local URL, back/forward, reload, open-external, and per-project remembered preview URL; it is clearly scoped as dev preview, not a general browser.

LPANE-MANAGER · Multiple agent panes per projectTerminal + process
v1🔒

Jason often runs multiple Claude/Codex sessions against the same project, and different open projects may run different agents at the same time. Add a pane manager that can create/switch/close multiple terminal panes per project.

Done criteria

One project can run 3 live agent/shell panes, and at least 2 open projects can run different agents concurrently; each pane has its own pty/process; focus is obvious; close/kill is explicit and safe.

MAGENT-SESSION-HANDLE · App-owned agent session handlesTerminal + process
v1🔒

Define the missing middle layer between real pty panes and the Codex-style composer: a stable app-owned handle for each pane/session.

Done criteria

Every pane/session exposes id, project/session ids, cwd, agent profile, process state, approval mode, send, interrupt, readTail, close, and activity metadata. Composer, activity log, and future hooks target the handle contract instead of UI component state.

MAGENT-ACTIVITY · Agent activity and thinking statesTerminal + process
v1

Background agents need Codex-style visible status and activity rows when they think, run commands, edit files, call tools, wait for approval/input, error, exit, or complete. Show concise activity/progress summaries and tool events, not hidden chain-of-thought.

Done criteria

Pane/project rail badges and pane headers show thinking/running-command/editing/waiting/error/exited/complete states; recent activity rows show events like Edited a file, Ran command, Command failed, Opened diff, Waiting for approval, and Complete; raw hidden reasoning is never displayed.

MAGENT-ACTIVITY-LOG · Agent activity timelineTerminal + process
v1🔒

Each pane/session needs an inspectable timeline of user-safe agent events so Jason can see what happened without reading every terminal line.

Done criteria

Per-pane/session log captures thinking/planning summaries, file read/edit/create/delete/rename events, command start/finish/failure with cwd and exit code, tool/app actions, git/source-control events, approval requests, errors, exits, and completion; events are attributed, filterable, retained with session metadata, and link to diffs/output/undo where available.

MAPP-ACTIONS-MINIMAL · Minimal app-owned action gateTerminal + process
v1🔒

Permission controls need real enforcement behind them. Add the small action gate for app-owned actions before showing Codex-style approval modes.

Done criteria

Focus pane, open file, open diff, attach screenshot/file reference, interrupt process, create pane, close pane, and open browser preview route through action descriptors with risk class, approval decision, audit event, and undo/rollback hint where possible.

MCOMPOSER-HARNESS · Composer harness controlsTerminal + process
v1🔒

Grow the bottom composer into a Codex-style app-owned harness around real terminal panes: permission mode, goal state, target pane, model/profile, attachments, and stop/send controls.

Done criteria

Composer supports Ask for approval, Approve safe actions, and Full access modes for app-owned actions; shows goal chip, target project/session/pane, model/profile selector, attachments/screenshot references with remove controls, stop/send state, and logs prompt/permission/attachment/action events to the activity timeline.

MSESSION-RESTORE · Restore projects, files, panesProjects + sessions
v1

After quit/relaunch, the cockpit should come back as the same working context, without pretending to preserve live agent process state.

Done criteria

Relaunch restores the left project rail, selected project/session, per-project file rail expansion, editor tabs, browser preview URL, pane layout, and starts fresh agent/shell panes where configured.

MDIFF-VIEW · Diff view for agent changesFiles + editor
v1

Jason supervises agents by reading what changed. A file status marker is not enough; he needs to inspect diffs without opening VS Code.

Done criteria

Modified files open a readable diff view with additions/deletions; the editor can jump from a diff hunk to the file.

SGIT-ACTIONS-LITE · Lightweight git review actionsFiles + editor
v1🔒

Add only the git actions needed to review/clean agent work, not a full git client.

Done criteria

Stage file, unstage file, discard file with confirmation, and copy diff are available from file status/diff surfaces.

Pebbles · features

SSAVE-ERROR-RECOVERY · Save failure recoveryFiles + editor
v0_5

Save failures need a recovery flow, not only a red message. The buffer must remain safe and the next action must be obvious.

Done criteria

Failed saves keep the edited buffer in place, show a scoped editor error with the human cause, expose explicit Retry and Open externally actions where applicable, avoid duplicate save spam while saving, and return to Saved only after the real write succeeds.

SEDITOR-RESPONSIVE-QA · Editor responsive screenshot QAQuality + shipping
v0_5

The userflow audit called out screenshot proof for editor states and small desktop widths before claiming parity.

Done criteria

Capture and keep QA artifacts for selected-file, dirty-file, save-error, no-file-selected, and 1024x640/narrow-width workbench states. Screenshots must show no overlapping chrome, no unusable squeezed editor, and clear rail/editor/terminal orientation.

SEDITOR-FIND-REPLACE · Editor find and replaceFiles + editor
v0_5

Basic file editing needs local find/replace; otherwise the editor is too thin to replace the VS Code surface Jason actually uses.

Done criteria

Find, next/previous result, replace one, and replace all work in the active editor without breaking terminal focus shortcuts.

SEDITOR-LANGUAGE-MODES · Editor language modesFiles + editor
v0_5

The first editor pass needs credible syntax support for the file types this repo and Jason's projects use most.

Done criteria

TypeScript, TSX/JSX, JavaScript, Markdown, HTML, CSS, Rust, JSON, TOML, YAML, and shell scripts open with syntax highlighting and sensible indentation defaults.

SLARGE-FILE-POLICY · Large and binary file policyFiles + editor
v0_5

The editor should fail safely on huge or binary files instead of freezing the app.

Done criteria

Large text files show a warning before full load; binary files show an explicit unsupported/preview state and never open as corrupt text.

MFILE-OPS · Basic file operationsFiles + editor
v0_5

Replacing the VS Code shell requires basic rail operations, not only open-file. These commands must be reachable from the rail, menu/shortcut paths, and the file context menu where relevant.

Done criteria

Create file, create folder, rename, delete, duplicate, and reveal in Finder work from the rail/context menu with confirmation for destructive operations.

SEDITOR-TABS · Open files and dirty-state tabsFiles + editor
v0_5

Let the user bounce between a few files without losing place. This is the minimum editor workflow, not a full IDE tab system.

Done criteria

Multiple open files appear as tabs; dirty files are marked; close prompts only when unsaved changes would be lost.

SAGENT-PROFILES · Agent command profilesTerminal + process
v0_5

Make Claude the default but stop hardcoding one command forever. Add simple profiles for Claude, Codex, and shell so the pane can launch the desired CLI.

Done criteria

New pane can choose Claude, Codex, or shell; command path/args are visible in config; missing commands fail with a useful message.

MAGENT-COMPOSER · Codex-style agent composerTerminal + process
v0_5

Keep terminal panes as the trusted source-of-truth agent interface, but add a bottom Codex-style composer for sending prompts/instructions to the selected real agent pane or triggering app-owned actions.

Done criteria

Composer can target the selected Claude/Codex/shell pane, send multiline text safely through the real pty, show target project/session/pane context, stop/interrupt the pane, retain draft/history, handle Enter/Shift+Enter/Escape predictably, and run app actions via command palette routing; it never replaces the underlying terminal transcript.

SSHORTCUTS · VS Code shortcut baselineChrome + interaction
v0_5

Keep VS Code muscle memory for the supported workflow: open folder, quick open/search, save, close, copy/paste, terminal focus, new pane, split/switch pane, and command palette.

Done criteria

A documented shortcut map covers the v0.5 workflow, matches VS Code defaults where applicable, appears in menu items/tooltips where relevant, and explicitly lists terminal/editor/app-chrome conflicts and exceptions.

MCONTEXT-MENUS · Right-click context menusChrome + interaction
v0_5

macOS pointer workflows need right-click/Control-click affordances, not only visible buttons. Context menus should expose the same command registry as menu bar, shortcuts, and command palette where possible.

Done criteria

Project rail, file explorer, editor tabs/text, terminal panes, browser preview, diff/git surfaces, and agent panes expose relevant context menus with disabled states, shortcut labels, and confirmation/undo for destructive actions; no command exists only in a hidden context menu.

MCHROME-POLISH-SYSTEM · Codex-level chrome polish systemChrome + interaction
v0_5

Define the workbench chrome system before more surfaces drift: dense rails, calm dark surfaces, tokens, menus, badges, pane headers, settings/sidebar rhythm, command palette, toasts, and restrained motion.

Done criteria

Core chrome uses semantic tokens, one icon family, compact desktop density, consistent active/hover/focus/disabled/loading/empty/error states, shared command labels across menus/tooltips/palette, accessible badges, and screenshot-based visual QA for one-project workbench, settings, context menu, command palette, and agent attention state.

MICON-SYSTEM · Codex-quality icon and status systemChrome + interaction
v0_5

The app should feel like a polished Codex-style workbench, not a raw prototype. Define one consistent icon language for project rail, file explorer, editor, browser, terminal, agent panes, git state, and common actions.

Done criteria

A single SVG/icon system covers primary actions and object types; selected states use filled/active treatment; non-universal icons have labels or tooltips; status badges cover thinking/running/waiting/error/exited/complete with accessible names.

SACCESSIBILITY-BASICS · Keyboard and focus accessibility baselineChrome + interaction
v0_5

A dense tool still needs visible focus, keyboard reachability, and labelled controls so it can be used without pointer precision.

Done criteria

Core chrome controls are keyboard reachable, focus rings are visible, and rail/editor/pane controls expose useful labels.

SPANE-NAMES · Pane names and task labelsTerminal + process
v1

When several agents run in one project, each pane needs a human label so Jason can remember what it is doing.

Done criteria

Each pane has an editable name/task label shown in the pane header and restored across relaunch.

MPROCESS-LIFECYCLE · Agent process lifecycle controlsTerminal + process
v1

A cockpit needs basic process affordances: restart, kill, status, exited state, and command/cwd visibility.

Done criteria

Each pane shows running/exited status, cwd, command profile, restart, and terminate. Exited panes keep scrollback until closed.

SDEV-SERVER-DETECT · Local dev server detectionChrome + interaction
v1

When Claude/Codex starts Vite, Next, Astro, or another dev server, the app should make the local preview obvious without acting like a full browser.

Done criteria

Detected localhost URLs from terminal output can be opened in the browser preview; the preview remembers the latest URL per project and offers reload/open-external.

MPERF-BUDGET · Resource and jank budgetQuality + shipping
v1

The product exists to avoid VS Code resource bloat. Measure the thing the product promises: memory, CPU, and responsiveness with several projects/panes.

Done criteria

Document baseline memory/CPU/jank for one-project edit+agent, two-agent same-project, three-project switch/relaunch, and fast terminal output runs; compare against the equivalent VS Code workflow and make regressions visible before release.

SDAILY-DRIVER-METRICS · Daily-driver success metricsQuality + shipping
v1

Define how to know the app is actually replacing the VS Code workflow instead of just existing.

Done criteria

Script and record three workflow runs: one-project edit+agent, two-agent same-project, and three-project switch/relaunch. Track steps, elapsed time, project opens, pane starts/stops, editor saves, crashes, memory/CPU snapshots, and whether VS Code was needed.

Sand · glue & polish

SGIT-STATUS · Git status in file railFiles + editor
v1

Small but high-signal replacement for a VS Code affordance Jason likely relies on: dirty/new/deleted markers in the file tree.

Done criteria

Rail marks modified, untracked, deleted, and ignored files for git workspaces without blocking UI on large repos.

Later

Rocks · foundational & risky

MAI-CONNECTIONS · AI connections and credentials settingsTerminal + process
v2

Users need to hook up real AI tooling: Claude/Codex CLI auth, provider API keys, model defaults, MCP servers, environment variables, permission defaults, and health checks.

Done criteria

Settings can configure Claude/Codex command profiles, provider/API credentials via OS-safe storage, MCP server entries, env vars, default models, permission policy, and validation checks that explain missing auth or broken connections.

MSOURCE-CONTROL-CONNECTIONS · GitHub and GitLab connectionsFiles + editor
v2

Local Git is not enough once agent work needs to move through PR/MR review. Add first-class source-hosting connections without becoming a full forge client.

Done criteria

Settings detects `git`, `gh`, and `glab` auth health; supports GitHub, GitLab.com, and self-hosted GitLab remotes; opens repo/PR/MR/issue/pipeline links in browser preview or external browser; shows source-host status for the active repo; credentials are stored only through OS-safe storage.

MAGENT-HOOKS · Built-in agent MCP/API hooksTerminal + process
v2

Give Claude/Codex-style agents a first-class way to cooperate with the app without making a plugin platform: inspect projects/panes/open files, request focus/split/new-pane actions, open diffs, and report task status through app-owned commands.

Done criteria

A local permissioned MCP/API endpoint exposes documented app-owned commands; actions that mutate files, git state, panes, or project layout require visible approval/undo where appropriate; every agent action is attributed in the UI/log.

MDIRECT-AGENT-HARNESS · Optional direct API agent harnessTerminal + process
v2

Research-backed future lane: app-owned direct API/MCP agent workflows using human review, resumable state, and tracing, without replacing real Claude/Codex terminal panes.

Done criteria

A feature-flagged direct agent can run app-owned tools/MCP with approval gates, state restore/resume, tracing/activity log entries, and clear separation from terminal-backed Claude/Codex panes.

Pebbles · features

MWORKTREE · Worktree helperTerminal + process
v2

One keystroke: new pane = git worktree add + agent launched inside it, named and disposable. The 'multiple agents on one repo, parallel' workflow.

Done criteria

From a project tab: new-worktree-agent in <5s; cleanup removes the worktree + branch.

MSEARCH · Find files and textFiles + editor
v2

A lightweight replacement for the VS Code search/file-jump behaviors that matter while supervising agents.

Done criteria

Keyboard-driven file quick-open and ripgrep-backed text search work inside the selected project.

MTERMINAL-FIND · Find in terminal scrollbackTerminal + process
v2

Agent output can be long. Search inside terminal scrollback is necessary for reviewing logs and prior agent output.

Done criteria

Find text in the active pane scrollback, jump next/previous, and preserve terminal focus behavior.

MSETTINGS-PARITY · Codex-style settings parityChrome + interaction
v2

Borrow Codex's searchable left-nav settings shape and icon density, but keep only categories that serve the lean VS Code replacement workflow.

Done criteria

Settings has searchable grouped sections for General, Appearance, App configuration, Behavior, Keyboard shortcuts, MCP servers, Browser preview, Agent hooks, Connections, Git, Environments, and Worktrees; Profile, Pets, Usage & billing, Archived chats, and Chat Settings are absent; Appshots and Computer use remain parked.

MPACKAGING · Local app packagingQuality + shipping
v3

Make it launch like a real macOS app, not only from `npm run tauri dev`.

Done criteria

A local signed or ad-hoc packaged .app launches, opens folders, and runs agents with documented setup requirements.

Sand · glue & polish

SCOMMAND-PALETTE · VS Code-style command paletteChrome + interaction
v2

A compact VS Code-style way to expose actions without adding IDE chrome: open folder, quick open, new agent pane, split, restart, kill, save, search, settings. It should share the same command registry as shortcuts and context menus.

Done criteria

A keyboard palette can run core app actions, shows shortcuts where assigned, and stays consistent with menu/context-menu labels.

SINTEGRATIONS-POLICY · Integration adapter policyQuality + shipping
v2

Define the allowed lane for other integrations so Bitbucket, Azure DevOps, Linear, Jira, Slack, or Discord do not turn the app into a plugin hub.

Done criteria

Documented integration policy classifies integrations as core, first-class, adapter-lane, parked, or out-of-scope; every integration requires a concrete workflow, health check, credential boundary, and app-owned command surface.

MSETTINGS · Settings for commands, themes, and shortcutsChrome + interaction
v2

Keep settings boring and inspectable: agent commands, AI connections, source-control connections, ignored folders, font, color theme, default layout, browser preview, hooks, git/worktree defaults, environments, and shortcut overrides. This is not a plugin platform.

Done criteria

A local config file controls agent profiles, AI connections, source-control connections, font/theme, ignored folders, default pane layout, browser preview, hooks, Git/worktree behavior, environments, and app-level keybindings; bad config reports actionable errors and plugins/extensions are not supported.

SNOTIFICATIONS · Background agent notificationsChrome + interaction
v2

When an agent in another project or pane stops or needs input, Jason should not have to manually inspect every pane.

Done criteria

Background panes can surface in-app badges and optional macOS notifications for exit/attention events.

STRANSCRIPTS · Pane transcript captureTerminal + process
v2

Completed agent sessions should be reviewable after the process exits without keeping panes open forever.

Done criteria

A pane can save a plain-text transcript/log path for later review, with clear retention/reset behavior.

SSESSION-ARCHIVE · Session archiveProjects + sessions
v2

Only after project sessions and transcripts exist, add a way to hide old sessions without turning the product into archived chats.

Done criteria

A completed project session can be archived/restored; archived sessions retain transcript references and metadata, stay out of the main rail, and are labeled as work sessions rather than chats.

SKEYBINDINGS-CONFIG · Configurable keybindingsChrome + interaction
v2

After the VS Code-compatible default shortcut map stabilizes, allow focused overrides without hardcoding personal preferences into the app.

Done criteria

A local config file can override app-level shortcuts, reports conflicts with terminal/editor reserved bindings, and generates a visible shortcut reference.

STHEME · Color themesChrome + interaction
v2

Support practical color themes across chrome, terminal, rail, and editor. Start with the approved mono-ghost theme; do not expand into plugin theming or break the chrome token contract.

Done criteria

App chrome, terminal canvas, file rail, and editor can switch between built-in color themes with readable contrast while preserving semantic token roles and component states.

SREUSE-AUDIT · Mine old projects for reusable piecesResearch & spikes
v3

Re-check Hallmark/hashmark/brutal/indx for reusable Tauri shell, persistence, editor, or design patterns without adopting their product shape.

Done criteria

Reusable components/patterns are listed with exact source paths and either imported, adapted, or explicitly rejected.

✅ Shipped (6)
MS-OSS · OSS landscape survey + tool evaluationsResearch & spikes
v0

July 2026: surveyed the field (zellij, Superconductor, hashmark, cmux, Mux0, Supacode). zellij trialed then parked (static-config model, not 'open a folder'). Superconductor/hashmark/cmux evaluated in depth. All reference only — the decision is build-our-own.

Done criteria

Drove the build-our-own direction. Rejected options documented in PARKED.md/DECISIONS.md with reasons. ✓ 2026-07-07

SS-EDITOR · Editor-fidelity spike (helix)Research & spikes
v0

hx sample.tsx next to the VSCode screenshot — confirmed a real editor with tree-sitter highlighting reads right. Informs the EDITOR slice (now CodeMirror in-app, not a TUI editor).

Done criteria

PASS, logged in DECISIONS.md. ✓ 2026-07-07

MS-AUDIT · Blind-spot audits (drift + fork decision)Research & spikes
v0

Two 16-framework /blind sweeps caught real drift: demo-replaced-the-trial, and an asymmetric 'cmux is best' verdict. Second audit corrected a false claim (cmux chrome IS themeable) and, with Jason's pushback, steered back to build-our-own. docs/blind-audit-*.html.

Done criteria

Findings logged in ERRORS.md; direction corrected. ✓ 2026-07-07

MS-SPIKE1 · libghostty-vt parsing spike — PASSEDTerminal + process
v0

spike-ghostty-vt/: real pty (portable-pty) → real 'ls -la -G' → real ANSI codes → libghostty-vt Terminal::vt_write parses → cell-by-cell readback matches exactly. Proves the terminal ENGINE choice (Ghostty's real parser in a Rust backend, not xterm.js). Caught the Zig 0.15.2 pin requirement.

Done criteria

Compiles + runs correctly, verified 2026-07-07. Logged in DECISIONS.md. ✓ 2026-07-07

LS-SPIKE2 · Tauri render + input loop — PASSEDTerminal + process
v0

spike-2/: real Tauri 2 + React/Vite + Rust app. pty → libghostty-vt → grid snapshot over IPC → Canvas 2D paint; browser key events → Ghostty encoder/compat mappings → pty. Includes Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, Option word navigation/delete, mouse drag selection, and Cmd+C copy from selected canvas text.

Done criteria

npm test, npm run build, and cargo test pass. Real app UI verification covered selection/Cmd+C, Cmd+V paste, Cmd+K clear, and Option-Backspace delete-word. ✓ 2026-07-08

MS-SCAFFOLD · Tauri 2 app scaffold — folded into SPIKE-2Terminal + process
v0

The scaffold was not a separate throwaway step: SPIKE-2 proved the real Tauri 2 + React/TS/Vite frontend, Rust backend, native window, IPC events, Tauri commands, plugins, and app menu accelerator path.

Done criteria

Native app opens, IPC is wired both directions, frontend and Rust builds pass. ✓ 2026-07-08
